title: ephedra andina var. humilis
intro: Ephedra andina var. humilis is a low-growing, shrubby plant species native to the high-altitude regions of South America, belonging to the Ephedraceae family. It is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly the presence of ephedrine and pseudoephedrine alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Ephedra andina var. humilis include its ability to increase oxygenation, boost energy, and aid in weight loss, making it a popular herbal supplement among athletes and individuals seeking to improve their physical performance. Ephedra andina var. humilis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including capsules, tablets, and teas, although its use is often subject to regulatory restrictions due to concerns over potential side effects.
